# dart_monty_core — index for LLM/agent consumers

Low-level Rust-backed sandboxed Python interpreter with Dart FFI
bindings. Smaller surface than `dart_monty`: no bridge, no
extensions, no host functions. Just `Monty.exec`, `Monty.typeCheck`,
`MontyLimits`, and the typed `MontyResult` wrapper.

If you're driving this package from an LLM agent, start here:

## Quick start

- `README.md` — overview + install
- `CHANGELOG.md` — release notes (subset rules in the "Notes"
  section of each release)
- `AGENTS.md` — conventions and contributor instructions
- `example/example.dart` — minimal end-to-end smoke test
- `lib/dart_monty_core.dart` — single-file public-API export

## What's the API

- `Monty.exec(code, limits?)` — one-shot execution, returns
  `MontyResult`. `code` is a Python expression or program.
- `Monty.typeCheck(code, prefixCode?)` — pre-flight type analysis
  using mypy-rules-of-thumb. Catches subset violations (no `class`,
  no decorators in core, etc.) BEFORE runtime.
- `MontyLimits(memoryBytes:, timeoutMs:)` — hard caps on the script.
  Out-of-memory and timeout violations surface as typed errors,
  NOT Dart-side OOMs or hangs.
- `MontyResult.value` — typed wrapper (`MontyInt`, `MontyString`,
  `MontyDouble`, `MontyBool`, `MontyList`, `MontyDict`, `MontyNull`).
  Pattern-match or call `.toJson()` for plain Dart values.

## Subset rules (read first)

- `class` (no user-defined classes) — use dicts and module-level
  functions
- generators (`yield`) — unsupported
- `match`/`case`, `del` — unsupported
- C extensions — unsupported
- decorators — supported (despite older docs claiming otherwise)
- walrus (`:=`), chained assignment — supported
- curated stdlib: `math`, `re`, `json`, `datetime`, `pathlib`,
  `collections`

## What's NOT on the doc surface

`lib/src/**`, `test/**`, `tool/**`, `hook/**`, and `native/**` are
implementation detail. If your question requires reading them, that
indicates a doc gap — file an issue or open a PR adding the missing
explanation.

## Companion package

`dart_monty` wraps this package with a Bridge, Extensions, and
host-function dispatch. If you need `MontyRuntime`, the template
engine, message bus, sandbox-spawn children, or anything beyond
`Monty.exec`, see `dart_monty/llms.txt`.
